titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 19 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). In this letter, Spender briefly discusses the bravery of General Powell in volunteering for the Air Force, the infighting among the General Staff, the progress of the fighting on the battlefield, and the work of Lillian's committee.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
June 19 My p r Very short comms
lately but hope this will
be some amends. Many thanks
for your orders for me. I hope
they may come soon so
that I can have them in
my dug out. What a fine old boy
Gen Powell really is in
volunteering for the Air
Service. When you write tell
him how splendid I think
it of him. P & I have been getting
on a little better lately, but
he had had beg rows with
the others which have been
hushed up partly for my
sake when I said I held myself
partly responsible. I do hope
this will be the last of such
trouble now. It is hard to understand
the Germans. Last night within
600 yards of their lines in
the open there were hundreds
of men waggons lorries
horses & mules â a terrible
but necessary risk & their
guns were silent. The
noise was like Piccadilly
at 11 p m If only we
can get through tonight
equally well with only a
bit of machine gun fire I
shall be relieved in mind. Your lists must be mounting
up & I'm afraid your
committee will be over working
themselves soon so keep your
self fit & look out for helpers
all round. Do not spare expense
on your health for the sake of this
Div & perhaps of yours truly who
still fails to get tired of the funny
on first page animal
he picked
off a
Canadian
dairy
farm W B Spender
